Tests for the `toString` method of `Number` objects in various bases and special cases.


## Files
- **[15.07.04.02-001.js](15.07.04.02-001.js.md)**: Tests the `toString` method of a `Number` object with base 36 conversion.
- **[15.07.04.02-003.js](15.07.04.02-003.js.md)**: Tests the `toString` method of a `Number` object for binary conversion.
- **[15.07.04.02-004.js](15.07.04.02-004.js.md)**: Tests the conversion of a large number to an octal string representation.
- **[15.07.04.02-005.js](15.07.04.02-005.js.md)**: Tests the conversion of a negative number to an octal string representation.
- **[15.07.04.02-006.js](15.07.04.02-006.js.md)**: Tests the `toString` method of `Number.prototype` with a base of 2.
- **[15.07.04.02-008.js](15.07.04.02-008.js.md)**: Tests the default string representation of the Number prototype in JavaScript.
- **[15.07.04.02-009.js](15.07.04.02-009.js.md)**: Tests if `Number.prototype` has a `toString` property that is a function.
- **[15.07.04.02-010.js](15.07.04.02-010.js.md)**: Tests the `toString` method of a `Number` object initialized with `NaN`.
- **[15.07.04.02-011.js](15.07.04.02-011.js.md)**: Tests the `toString` method of a `Number` object with `NaN` and `undefined` as arguments.
- **[15.07.04.02-012.js](15.07.04.02-012.js.md)**: Tests the `toString` method of a `Number` object with `Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY`.
- **[15.07.04.02-013.js](15.07.04.02-013.js.md)**: Tests the `toString` method of a `Number` object with `Number.NEGATIVE_INFINITY`.
